name: Manjushri
mantraText: Om Ah Ra Pa Tsa Na Dhih
openingText:
མཚན་དཔེའི་དཔལ་འབར་ཡིད་འོང་གཞོན་ནུའི་སྐུ།
Tsen pé pal bar yi ong zhön nü ku
Your beautiful and youthful body blazes in splendour with the major and minor marks

ཚངས་དབྱངས་གསུང་གིས་ཟབ་རྒྱས་ཆོས་ཚུལ་སྒྲོགས།
Tsang yang sung gi zab gyé chö tsul drok
Your speech proclaims the profound and vast Dharma in the divine voice of Brahmā

མཁྱེན་པ་གཉིས་ལྡན་དམིགས་མེད་སྙིང་རྗེའི་ཐུགས།
Khyen pa nyi den mik mé nying jé tuk
And your mind possesses non-referential compassion endowed with twofold wisdom

མགོན་པོ་འཇམ་པའི་དབྱངས་ལ་ཕྱག་འཚལ་ལོ།
Gön po jam pé yang la chak tsal lo
Protector Mañjughoṣa, to you I pay homage
closingText:
དགེ་བ་འདི་ཡིས་མྱུར་དུ་བདག
Gé wa di yi nyur du dag
By this merit, may I swiftly

འཇམ་དཔལ་དབྱངས་དབང་འགྲུབ་གྱུར་ནས།
Jam pel yang wang drub gyur né
Attain the state of Manjushri

འགྲོ་བ་གཅིག་ཀྱང་མ་ལུས་པ།
Dro wa chik kyang ma lü pa
And may every single living being without exception

དེ་དག་ས་ལ་འགོད་པར་ཤོག།
Dé dak sa la gö par shog
Be established in that very same level.
deityInfo:
MANJUSHRI - The Bodhisattva of Wisdom

Significance:
Manjushri embodies the wisdom of all Buddhas. His name means 'Gentle Glory' or 'Sweetly Splendid'. He holds a sword that cuts through ignorance and a scripture representing the perfection of wisdom.

Key Qualities:
• Supreme wisdom and intellect
• Eloquence and memorization
• Understanding of emptiness
• Sharp discrimination
• Mastery of Dharma

The Mantra: OM AH RA PA TSA NA DHIH
This mantra enhances wisdom and understanding:
• OM - Primordial sound of enlightenment
• AH - Wisdom realizing the nature of reality
• RA - Wisdom seeing the purity of all things
• PA - Wisdom as the source of all qualities
• TSA - Wisdom clearly manifesting
• NA - Wisdom of the unchanging ultimate nature
• DHIH - Seed of Manjushri's wisdom—grants intelligence and understanding

Visualization:
Manjushri appears orange in color. His right hand holds a flaming sword of wisdom raised in the air. His left hand holds a blue lotus at his heart, upon which rests the Prajnaparamita (Perfection of Wisdom) scripture. He sits on a moon disc atop a lotus throne.

The Sword of Wisdom:
The sword cuts through:
• Ignorance and delusion
• Dualistic thinking
• Attachment to concepts
• The root of samsara

Benefits of Practice:
• Increases intelligence and memory
• Improves understanding of Dharma
• Helps pass exams and learn new skills
• Clarifies thinking and decision making
• Cuts through confusion and doubt

Famous Emanations:
• Tsongkhapa (founder of Gelug tradition)
• Longchenpa (great Nyingma scholar)
• Sakya Pandita Kunga Gyaltsen (Sakya tradition)

Visualization Practice:
From Manjushri's heart, light radiates to you and all beings, dispelling darkness and bestowing the gift of Great Wisdom.

Recitation Advice:
Reciting the mantra 108 times daily enhances memory. 1000 times sharpens intellect. Reciting the mantra 100,000 times leads to deeper realizations.

stages.name: Main Practice
stages.mantraText: Om Ah Ra Pa Tsa Na Dhih
